commit.message:
ci: skip Node.js/UI build for wfctl and lsp-server binary builds (#378)

* Initial plan

* ci: skip Node.js and UI build for non-server binaries in release matrix

The build matrix in release.yml was running the Node.js setup and UI asset
build steps for all binary types (server, wfctl, lsp-server). The UI assets
are only required for the server binary since it uses Go embed to serve the
admin UI.

Add build_ui: true to the server matrix entry and guard the Node.js setup
and UI build steps with `if: matrix.build_ui`. The wfctl and lsp-server
entries omit the flag (evaluates to falsy) so those steps are skipped,
saving unnecessary work on each build.

The 10+ minute build times for server and wfctl are due to sequential
cross-compilation across 5 target platforms (linux/amd64, linux/arm64,
darwin/amd64, darwin/arm64, windows/amd64), each taking ~3 minutes. This
is expected behavior; the Go build cache (823 MB) is restored but does not
include cross-compiled artifacts for non-native targets.

Co-authored-by: intel352 <77607+intel352@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(protocol): preserve registry entry order with IndexMap

The RegistryMap inner type was HashMap<String, serde_json::Value> which
has non-deterministic iteration order (Rust's RandomState). When
minecraft:dimension_type entries were sent in random order during
configuration, the hardcoded dimension_type_id=0 in the login packet
would randomly map to the wrong dimension type (e.g. the_nether with
min_y=0 and no skylight instead of overworld with min_y=-64).

This caused intermittent startup issues where:
- Chunks appeared to start from Y=0 instead of Y=-64
- Skybox rendering was wrong
- Blocks were not breakable

The fix:
1. Replace inner HashMap with IndexMap to preserve JSON insertion order
   (matching vanilla's registration order)
2. Add get_registry_entry_index() to look up entry indices by name
3. Replace hardcoded dimension_type_id=0 with dynamic lookup

BREAKING CHANGE: indexmap workspace dep now requires serde feature

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix freeform response loop in CI failure and comment flows (#45)

* Fix freeform response loop in CI failure and comment flows

When a user types freeform text instead of picking a choice,
ProcessTaskComplete had no check for CiFailureFlowState.InvestigationResults
or CommentFlowState.MultiCommentPrompt, causing it to fall through to
TransitionToPolling. The poll then re-detected the same terminal state
and restarted the flow from scratch, creating an infinite loop.

Now ProcessTaskComplete re-presents the original choices after freeform
task_complete for all active flows. Also made BuildFreeformInterpretAction
flow-aware so CI failures use push_completed (not comment_addressed).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Handle push_completed from ExecutingTask for freeform CI fixes

Add (ExecutingTask, push_completed) => TransitionToPolling handler so
freeform CI Path B code changes correctly transition to polling instead
of hitting unexpected state recovery.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Distinguish reply vs non-reply freeform in comment flow

When a user's freeform instruction is to reply without code changes
(pushback/clarification), use comment_replied with reply_text instead
of task_complete so the reply gets posted and the comment flow
transitions correctly.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update SKILL.md freeform docs for comment_replied and push_completed

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix bracket matching in interpolated strings when { is at end of line

When { is the last character on a line inside an interpolated string
(e.g. newline directly after {), bracket matching breaks because:

1. The interpolated-string end pattern matches { at end-of-line via
   [^\\\n]$, terminating the string as invalid before the interpolation
   child pattern can begin.
2. The interpolation begin pattern uses a positive lookahead (?=[^\{])
   which requires a character to exist after {, failing at end-of-line.

Fix:
- Exclude { from the interpolated-string end pattern so it cannot
  compete with the interpolation begin at the same position.
- Change interpolation/raw-interpolation/double-raw-interpolation begin
  patterns from positive lookahead (?=[^\{]) to negative lookahead
  (?!\{), which correctly succeeds at end-of-line.

Fixes dotnet/vscode-csharp#9077
